post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 198: Ice Cream Mafia


Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!

  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 198
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Flavor idea came from Charge
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are Vanilla Ice Cream. You have no other power other than your vote.

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
Cop wrote:
You are Cookies and Cream Ice Cream (Cop). Every night you may dip one of your cookies in another ice cream to sample their qualities (determine alignment).

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
Jailkeeper wrote:
You are Cookie Dough Ice Cream (Jailkeeper). Every night you may cover another ice cream with dough, preventing them from moving or being attacked.

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
Tracker wrote:
You are Mint Chocolate Chip Ice Cream (Tracker). Every night you may put some mint on another ice cream and track their movements through smell.

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
Doctor wrote:
You are Garlic Ice Cream (Doctor). Every night you may put some garlic on another ice cream so they will not be eaten.

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
1 Shot Bulletproof wrote:
You are Pistachio Ice Cream (1 Shot Bulletproof Townie). Since you are a reviled flavor of ice cream, it takes two nights for you to be eaten.

You win when all of the evil ice cream (Mafia) has been eliminated.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are Chocolate Ice Cream (Mafia Goon). Each night you may talk with your partner in crime here and arrange for an ice cream to be eaten.

You win when all of the good ice cream (Town) has been eliminated.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are Neapolitan Ice Cream (Mafia Roleblocker). Each night you may prevent someone from taking an action. Also, you may talk with your partner in crime here and arrange for an ice cream to be eaten.

You win when all of the good ice cream (Town) has been eliminated.


Alive (5)
+mathjay
+Charge
+atomicpianowitch avanders
TheFieryFalcon Made_in_2004
J5155 asdf334 xzhang


Dead(3)
cw357 (1 shot Bulletproof)
midwaybear (Vanilla Townie)
cherryolive (Mafia Roleblocker)
Mathletesv (Vanilla Townie)


Events
Day 1: cw357 hanged
Night 1: midwaybear dies
Day 2: cherryolive hanged
Night 2: No one died
Day 3: Mathletesv hanged
Night 3: No one died


PM Prods
 


Warnings
cherryolive: editing
asdf334: OOTing
cherryolive: OOT
asdf334: OOT


Some Mafia Guides
https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c488316_mafia_university


Story
There was an ice cream shop with a large variety of ice cream. But one day, the evil chocolate ice cream decided to try to get rid of all of the other ice cream in the shop, by having them all be eaten.


Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins. Current time: 18:00:00 EDT 23 March 2020.

post_rendered:
Formal post here.
So I'm one of your SEs, as is mathjay and, oh, apw?
Not everyone is a fan of fdas's guides, but I advise that every newbie go and take a look at them.
(!fdas can you link the mafia university forum into the OP?)
Standard D1 often follows RVS/RQS -> scumhunting, although it is fine if we skip RVS, I suppose.
As for RQS, I believe parts of it are important, like time zone, and meta diving (which means to go back and look at older games from experienced players).
Therefore, RQS:
1. Preferred pronoun(s)?
2. Time zone?
3. Expected Activity?
4. Good & recent town & scum game, if any? (note that 3p games aren't necessary here...)
My Answers
1. N/A; you can call me whatever.
2. MST
3. I am usually pretty active in the beginning phases of the game, but my activity drops afterwards.
4. Town: 185, Scum: 192

So a couple notes about mafia for the newbies who don't read guides (which is probably most of you)

1. Avoid excessive fluff. (Do as I say, not as I do... on that note, does anyone play rhythm games?)
2. Avoid bandwagoning and sheeping, please. This is central to eliminating the current TWTBAW playstyle.
3. I personally think that (blind) tunneling is bad. However, fdas (mod) tunnels a lot even though he is forum mod...
4. Read the actual thread. We don't need players who don't read, thanks. (I think most people do read, though.)
5. Note special rules. eg Deadline Hang/Majority Hang (fdas uses Majority Hang 99.9% of the time, so DO NOT HAMMER EARLY OR FORGET TO HAMMER AT DEADLINE)
For those of you who answered that you are pretty active.
Ha!
We'll see when you get your PM prods a few days later...
@mathjay, @mathjay, @mathjay, AoPS doesn't ping you even if I @ you...
also I personally don't think nicknames are necessary, although others might disagree
also @mathjay, note that some flavor credit goes to me. However, I am somewhat ashamed of how fdas implemented it... and have arguably insulted him in discord over it

post_rendered:
there are a lot of different ways
but the way i personally (used to) do it is
look at someone's posts
think about whether they would do it if they were scum, and how it would benefit them
then think about whether they would do it if they were town, and how it would benefit them in that case
and compare them
if you think it would be better for them to do it as scum, then its scummy
if you think it would be better for them to do it as town, then its towny
this way of scumhunting is based on motivations and it can really help when youre starting out

post_rendered:
yea
something i forgot to say by the way is the concept of "NAI," which means Not Alignment Indicative
it literally means something could be done by both town and scum and its kinda difficult to tell which
for example, theoretically, if someone in this game was getting into a lot of arguments, you could think
a) wow, this person is creating a lot of chaos and making it hard for people to tell what's happening. since town wants to observe carefully and not miss details, this person could be scum trying to make it hard for town to read people. or maybe it's even a distraction or something
but alternatively you could also think
b) people get into arguments all the time, and maybe arguing is something they do a lot of. maybe they just genuinely disagree with a lot of people, and since theyre invested in this game and want to win, theyre trying to discuss stuff with people - which is towny because discussions help town a lot, talking with someone could help you figure out something that you missed - and the discussions just got out of hand
maybe this is a bad example, but this could be NAI - you can see both town and scum motivations for it, and neither case is particularly stronger than the other.
or maybe neither motivation is strong enough - like, it could i guess[/b] be good for scum, but it's not really that good or necessary and town do it all the time, so it doesn't really matter and it's not important enough for me to make a read off of that

sry thats probably hard to read, but in summary, not everything has to be scum-or-town. there are lots of things that aren't really important, so think about which "evidence" you really think is important and which you think dont matter that much
or if you have trouble deciding, just post your thoughts and we can work through them together

P.S.  Part of my reasoning in my posts has been trying to maintain a communal exploration and discovery mindset versus an me vs. you mindset.  Although the latter certainly is helpful and important later the game, the former appears to be preferable for now.

cw357's read on cherryolive
General Thoughts
I know that almost all of us have previously suspected cherryolive, so I don't want this to come across as bandwagoning.
Instead, I wanted to bring up some unmentioned points that I noticed when reading cherryolive's posts. Throughout the entire game, cherryolive seems to be directing attention to other players and away from himself/herself. A form of bandwagoning, cherryolive did this most recently in post 229, Exhibit A, in which he bandwagoned on my previous post while describing another player as suspicious. This, however, could just be the mark of an analytical townsperson, though other evidence demonstrates cherryolive's efforts to shift attention onto other players. The most glaringly obvious and yet subtle example of this is post 201, Exhibit B. At that point, cherryolive was regarded as one of the most suspicious players of the game. Cherryolive's bringing up of the concept of too towny to be a town, while outwardly innocent, could be an effort to start investigations on other towny players while drawing attention away from himself/herself. Finally, posts 198-201 in general illustrate cherryolive's efforts to undermine evidence against himself/herself, all while trying to steer reads in the direction of other players.

Especially considering that someone's playstyle may differ from game to game for reasons other than alignment. For instance, if the situation in two games is different, you can reasonably expect someone to have played differently.

Mafia know who the other mafia are though, so why would I say mathjay was scummy if we were both mafia?

I hope you can see the paradox this creates called WIFOM (wine in front of me). Essentially, mafia can scumread their partner, and get called town because they scumread scum, and mafia wouldn’t scumread their partner. However, then all the mafia would start doing it, because you can get townread for it, even though it’s throwing your partner under the bus. But then, all of a sudden, it loses its townread potential, because the mafia all start doing it. So then, since scumreading your mafia partner is no longer towny, the mafia don’t do it anymore. Which makes it towny.

So the short answer is, you saying mathjay is scummy doesn’t really mean anything for whether you guys are partners. Reading based off interaction is pretty advanced, and usually goes beyond who was scumreading who.

You read mathjay as scummy, but is there anyone you see as town?

post_rendered:
I do acknowledge that my frequent discussion of J5155 could come across as tunneling, or worse, bandwagoning. Although it likely was short-sighted of me to confine attention to J5, the following three things were my intentions:

1) Discuss suspicions about J5 - this one is kind of obvious.
2) Somewhat a reaction test - I decided that since I was on a bandwagon, I might as well stay on it to see who else became involved.
3) I was discussing the potential of hanging J5 if no better evidence surfaced - forgive me for uttering such a terrible phrase mathjay, but J5 seemed like a "safe hang" if we couldn't find anyone better. Granted, it looks like that someone hung will be me based on recent discussion.. (WAIT NO I DIDN'T MEAN TO SAY THAT )

I acknowledge that my obsession with a "safe hang" in those posts does seem scummy, but I brought it up only for the purposes of decision-making if no better evidence surfaced come the deadline, and also with the recognition that continuing to look for evidence concerning other players was still very important, so that hanging J5 would not be necessary.

DADV stands for Dead Air, Dead Villager. Essentially, if someone is gathering a lot of momentum for a hang, but no one is defending them/townreading them, and there's not a long of super strong support for them, they're usually a villager.

As for my overall thoughts:
For the hang I'm leaning towards J5. I know I was opposed to him before, but he really hasn't done anything towny. His lurking is not the scummiest part, it's the bandwagoning. Just look at his last two posts: There's no original thoughts. Nothing that comes from a town perspective. But like I said, it feels DADV, so I'm hesitant to go ahead and hang him. Which is why I'm looking to read further into cherryolive.
Now cherry had that towny conversation with me, idk 5 days ago or so. I thought cherry was really towny there, I was able to get a lot of her original thoughts and her opinions seemed genuine. I gotta say I haven't read her hard enough though, I can't really remember a whole lot of what she's done besides some stuff in the early game.
midway is the last major hang candidate. I was pretty convinced that he was scum before, because of bandwagoning, cracking under pressure, backtracking, staying in the background, etc. But I really think his recent posts have been towny. He's been trying to solve the game, and moving us forward. Read #678 and #682. Another towny thing about him recently is that he's positive in some things, where he has looked into it, but he still questions things where he's not sure.

As for the other players?
Fieryfalcon was scummy early, and Madein hasn't really showed us much. I think he's someone we gotta look further into D2. All of his posts so far D1 have been safe, and replicatable by scum, but there's nothing that has been inherently scummy, either.
Mathlete I've got a strong townread on you. Your attitude early game was really towny, and the early game is where newbie scum usually doesn't know how to act towny. Your reaction tests were unnecessary, which is exactly what made them towny. Your lurking was slightly scummy, but the attitude you have come back into the game with is towny, too. post_rendered:
I recently got hit with a bunch of assignments (certainly the wrong time!), but here are my thoughts and a defense to midwaybear, apw, and mathjay's accusations.

A Defense to Midwaybears #737 and apw and mathjays accusations
Regarding voting myself, please do not read into this too much. I was essentially doing an OMGUS vote on myself as a troll. Regarding my rapid shift from "J5 is a risky hang" to "Let's hang J5!" in a short amount of time:
1) Some of my statemtns regarding J5 were part of the reaction test.
2) I had recently read through the game and was concerned we had started stalling in scumhunting (how wrong I was!).
This is essentially my progression:
1) I thought that given J5's few posts, he/she would be a risky hang.
2) I read through the game, and realized that we were starting to stall in scumhunting. (I don't want this to seem like a newbcard, but I didn't realize how much progress we could still make in a day.)
3) I did a reaction test to see if I could incite action in scumhunting.
4) After reading opinions from SE's on J5 and the danger of a "safe hang" mindset, I agreed.

Thoughts on the Hang Pool
cw357
It looks like I am now part of the hang pool , which I legitimately find hard to understand, except for my J5 progression which I just explained.

midwaybear
To be honest, midwaybear was one of my top scum candidates once he/she became a little more active. His/her post #758 convinced me however that because I was so focused on scumhunting, I was not realizing the NAI nature of your posts. Still a decent hang candidate.

The SEs
Frankly, I don't feel comfortable hanging any of the SEs, since if they flip town, we will have lost a lot of contribution.

Made_in_2004 (TheFieryFalcon)
Made_in_2004 is my top scum candidate. TheFieryFalcon started out fairly scummy, and upon being replaced, it seems like Made_in_2004, a more experienced people, is trying to cover up his/her shady past through agreement, while still not making real contribution (a scum trait).

Mathletesv
I am extremely concerned about Mathletesv. Although I don't feel confident hanging him/her because he/she might just have no original ideas on the game, I think we have let Mathletesv bandwagon and escape notice.

J5155
I really don't know what to think of J5, but he/she has contributed so little that we probably shouldn't take a chance on him/her.

In summary, I think Made_in_2004 is the best hang.

Unvote
Vote: Made_in_2004

post_rendered:
Suppose mafia killed a vanilla townie last night.
The jailkeeper can claim with who he targeted last night. Now there are two clears and 3 non-clears. This is the exact same scenario here, where town has a 100% chance of winning:
Quote:
Let us imagine a scenario taking place in a Matrix6 game, where a Vanilla Townie was hanged the first day, a Vanilla townie died the first night, a mafia goon was hanged the second day, and a vanilla townie died the second night. There are now 5 players, one of whom is a mafia. On day 3, the Jailkeeper claimed to have jailed player A on night 1 and player B on night 2, both of whom were still alive.

Now, the jailing on player A does not give us definitive information, because his partner could have submitted the kill on night 1. But on night 2, there was only 1 mafia left. Thus, if mafia was successfully jailed, he could not have performed the kill. And there was a kill on night 2, so this would seemingly clear player B. However, if there was a mafia roleblocker, since RB takes precedence of JK, a roleblocker could have been jailed and still performed the kill if he blocked the JK. Of course, there was only 1/6 probability of the RBer choosing the JK on night 2 since JK had not yet claimed, and this would make player B very likely to be town. However, let us focus only on absolutes for now.

The Jailkeeper claim is confirmed. JK is a verifiable claim in matrix6 and town still has a mishang. So, any fake claim would be cced and town could hang both competing claims and win.

Thus, there are only 2 setups possible.
Setup A: Jailkeeper, 1-Shot Bulletproof, Mafia Roleblocker
Setup 1: Jailkeeper
The game was in fact setup 1, but the players do not know this yet.

If this were setup 1, then Player B would be confirmed town.
Moreover, if it were known for sure that it was setup 1, then town has a strategy guaranteed to win them the game.
Both Jailkeeper and Player B are confirmed town. There are 3 unconfirmed players, call them X, Y, Z.
First, have jailkeeper declare that they will jail player Y. Then, hang player X.
If player X is mafia, town wins. Otherwise, either Y or Z is mafia.
If Y was mafia, when day starts no one is dead. Hang Y and plan to jail Z. Town wins.
If Z was mafia, and decided to not kill someone, the situation looks identical to if Y was mafia. Town hangs Y and jails Z. The next night, Z is jailed and no one dies. Then town hangs Z and wins.
If Z was mafia and killed someone, then Y is now confirmed town, since Y could not have performed the kill if jailed. Now, Z is the only remaining suspect. Hang Z and win.

Moreover, if it were known for sure that it was setup 1, then town has a strategy guaranteed to win them the game.
Both Jailkeeper and Player B are confirmed town. There are 3 unconfirmed players, call them X, Y, Z.
First, have jailkeeper declare that they will jail player Y. Then, hang player X.
If player X is mafia, town wins. Otherwise, either Y or Z is mafia.
If Y was mafia, when day starts no one is dead. Hang Y and plan to jail Z. Town wins.
If Z was mafia, and decided to not kill someone, the situation looks identical to if Y was mafia. Town hangs Y and jails Z. The next night, Z is jailed and no one dies. Then town hangs Z and wins.
If Z was mafia and killed someone, then Y is now confirmed town, since Y could not have performed the kill if jailed. Now, Z is the only remaining suspect. Hang Z and win.[/quote]

post_rendered:
Mechanical analysis
On night 2, it would have been optimal play for mafia to no kill, but on night 3 mafia should have tried to kill jailkeeper.

As it was pointed out, if mafia killed a VT night 2, then the jailkeeper could claim and clear the person he jailed as well as himself. Next, the jailkeeper announces who he will jail next who also becomes a clear if the mafia kills on that night. With 3 conf towns and 5 alive, the town win. If the mafia try to no kill after this to avoid the generation of the extra clear, then the 2 conf towns will never die and the game is cleared also.

So, on night 2 mafia had a 2/3 chance of outright losing the game (if they shot someone who was neither the jailkeeper or the jail target)

On night 3, mafia had a 1/5 chance of killing the JK and a 1/5 chance of failing the kill due to hitting the jail target. So there is a 3/5 chance of an outright loss.

Therefore, waiting one night will give the mafia a higher chance of winning, while also not increasing the number of mishangs the town has since no killing for one night would only make the playercount even.

However, by no killing 2 nights in a row, the mafia will give the town an extra hang and thus make it harder for the mafia to avoid the hang. The smaller POE to find JK is not worth the difficulty in surviving an additional hang. So, the mafia should have no killed night 2 and killed n3. Of course, this analysis assumes that the mafia is not jailed, since if the mafia is jailed their actions don't matter and there is no point in thinking about what to do. And in this case the mafia was jailed so it ultimately didn't matter.
post_canonical:
Mechanical analysis
On night 2, it would have been optimal play for mafia to no kill, but on night 3 mafia should have tried to kill jailkeeper.

As it was pointed out, if mafia killed a VT night 2, then the jailkeeper could claim and clear the person he jailed as well as himself. Next, the jailkeeper announces who he will jail next who also becomes a clear if the mafia kills on that night. With 3 conf towns and 5 alive, the town win. If the mafia try to no kill after this to avoid the generation of the extra clear, then the 2 conf towns will never die and the game is cleared also.

So, on night 2 mafia had a 2/3 chance of outright losing the game (if they shot someone who was neither the jailkeeper or the jail target)

On night 3, mafia had a 1/5 chance of killing the JK and a 1/5 chance of failing the kill due to hitting the jail target. So there is a 3/5 chance of an outright loss.

Therefore, waiting one night will give the mafia a higher chance of winning, while also not increasing the number of mishangs the town has since no killing for one night would only make the playercount even.

However, by no killing 2 nights in a row, the mafia will give the town an extra hang and thus make it harder for the mafia to avoid the hang. The smaller POE to find JK is not worth the difficulty in surviving an additional hang. So, the mafia should have no killed night 2 and killed n3. Of course, this analysis assumes that the mafia is not jailed, since if the mafia is jailed their actions don't matter and there is no point in thinking about what to do. And in this case the mafia was jailed so it ultimately didn't matter.
